readiness_rationale: Audit tooling (13 modules) is the strongest subsystem and is genuinely functional. Crawler, simulation, orchestration and repair are skeleton or empty. No end-to-end pipeline exists. No LOGOS target config is present. About 38% of required ARCHON_PRIME infrastructure is operational.

missing_modules.expected: Pipeline stage module connecting crawl_engine → audit_tools
missing_modules.impact: Crawler cannot feed audit tools automatically

missing_modules.expected: Repair module applying fixes from audit results

missing_modules.impact: Cannot simulate runtime behaviour before deployment
Cannot simulate import surface before crawl

missing_modules.expected: Task routing module dispatching jobs between subsystems
missing_modules.impact: Subsystems are isolated — cannot chain end-to-end

partial_modules.issue: Prints to stdout only — no JSON output, no configurable root, no pipeline integration
Infinite sleep loop printing to stdout — no actual state polling or progress reporting
Counts .py files and prints — no simulation model, no JSON output
Passthrough only — copies import_map.json verbatim without graph analysis

unwired_pipeline_segments: simulation → any other subsystem not wired
No end-to-end runner exists for the full ARCHON_PRIME pipeline
metadata_inconsistencies: 0 of 93 files contain all 6 required metadata fields (artifact_id, system, purpose, author, authority, timestamp)
audit_utils.py header format uses date M-D-YYYY, not ISO8601
logs/ directory artifacts have no header structure (flat arrays or NDJSON)
governance_coverage_map.py 'file' key missing from issues dict — write_log files_affected will be 0
logos_readiness_blockers: Crawler cannot accept LOGOS repo as configurable target
No repair capability to act on audit findings
